title: 第 72 節
en: 8. Apurvya (Rv. VI, 32)
9. Ya ogish/kak (Rv. VI, 33) 10. Sam ka tve (Rv. VI, 34) n. Kada bhuvan (Rv. VI, 35)
12. Satra madasa^ (Rv. VI, 36)
13. Arvag ratham (Rv. VI, 37)
14. Apad(Rv.VI, 38) .
15. Katha mahan (Rv. IV, 23)
16. Indro madaya (Rv. I, 81, 1)
Though there are said to be 100 verses before the Pahkti (No. 16), I can get only 99 or 101. See the following note.
2 The right wing is deficient by one verse, the left wing exceeds by one verse. I count 99 or 101 verses in the right, and 100 or 102 in the left wing.
I ARAiVYAKA, \ ADHYAYA, 2 KHANDA, II. I 9 I 6. Next comes the Sudadohas verse. Sudadohas is breath, and thereby he joins all joints with breath.
7. Next follows the tail. They are twenty-one Dvipada verses \ For there are twenty-one backward feathers in a bird.
8. Then the Ekavi;;^a is the support of all Stomas, and the tail the support of all birds 2.

9. He recites a twenty-second verse. This is made the form of two supports. Therefore all birds support themselves on their tail, and having supported themselves on their tail, they fly up. For the tail is a support.

10. He (the bird and the hymn) is supported by two decades which are Viraf. The man (the sacrificer) is supported by the two Dvipadas, the twentyfirst and twenty-second. That which forms the bird serves for the attainment of all desires ; that which forms the man, serves for his happiness, glory, proper food, and honour.

11. Next comes a Sudadohas verse, then a Dhayya, then a Sudadohas verse. The Sudadohas is a man, the Dhayya a woman, therefore he recites the Dhayya as embraced on both sides by the Sudadohas. Therefore does the seed of both, when it is effused, obtain oneness, and this with regard to the 1 These verses are given Ait. Ar. V, 2, 2, 9.

1. Ima nu kam (Rv. X, 157) .... 5 2. A yahi (Rv. X, 172) 4 3. Pra va indraya &c. (not in the -Sakalya-sawzhita) 9 4. Esha brahma &c. (not in the .Sakalya-sazwhita) _3_ 2 The other Stomas of the Agnish/oma are the Trivn't, PaS/cada^a, Saptada^a, the Ekaviw^a being the highest. Cf. I, 5, 1, 3.
192 AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.
woman only. Hence birth takes place in and from the woman. Therefore he recites that Dhayya in that place1.
Third Khanda.

1. He recites the eighty tristichs of Gayatris 2. Verily, the eighty Gayatri tristichs are this world (earth). Whatever there is in this world of glory, greatness, wives, food, and honour, may I obtain it, may I win it, may it be mine.
2. Next comes the Sudadohas verse. Sudadohas verily is breath. He joins this world with breath.

3. He recites the eighty tristichs of Br/hatis. Verily, the eighty Brz'hati tristichs are the world of the sky. Whatever there is in the world of the sky of glory, greatness, wives, food, and honour, may I obtain it, may I win it, may it be mine.
4. Next comes the Sudadohas verse. Sudadohas verily is breath. He joins the world of the sky with breath.

5. He recites the eighty tristichs of Ush/nh. Verily, the eighty Ushwih tristichs are that world, the heaven. Whatever there is in that world of glory, greatness, wives, food, and honour, also the divine being of the Devas (Brahman), may I obtain it, may I win it, may it be mine.
6. Next comes the Sudadohas verse. Sudadohas verily is the breath. He joins that world with breath, yea, with breath.
1 Asmin vi^-avabhage. Comm.
2 These and the following verses form the food of the bird. Comm. The verses themselves are given by -Saunaka in the fifth Ara»yaka.
First Kkanda.
1. He recites the Vasa hymn1, wishing, May everything be in my power.
zh: 8. 阿普爾維亞（梨俱吠陀 VI, 32）
9. 雅欧吉什/卡克（梨俱吠陀卷六，讚歌33） 10. 桑卡特維（梨俱吠陀卷六，讚歌34） 11. 卡達布旺（梨俱吠陀卷六，讚歌35）
12. Satra madasa^（梨俱吠陀 VI, 36）
13. Arvag ratham（梨俱吠陀‧六‧三七）
14. 阿帕德（梵 Apad）（《梨俱吠陀》第六卷，第38讚歌）。
15. 卡塔‧瑪罕（梵：Katha mahan，《梨俱吠陀》第四卷第23首讚歌）
16. 因陀羅啊，請為我們增長（梵：Indro madaya）（《梨俱吠陀》I, 81, 1）
雖然據說帕赫蒂經（第16號）之前有100首頌詩，但我只能找到99首或101首。見下面的註釋。
右翼缺少一句頌文，左翼則超出一句頌文。我在右翼數出99或101句頌文，在左翼則數出100或102句頌文。
《阿蘭尼亞卡》（Aranyaka）、《阿迪亞亞》（Adhyaya）、《康達》（Khanda）第二卷，第一九一六節。接著是蘇陀陀罕斯頌（Sudadohas verse）。蘇陀陀罕斯是呼吸，透過呼吸，他以氣息連結所有的關節。
接著是尾部。這是二十一首雙行詩頌（Dvipada verses）。因為鳥類有二十一根向後的羽毛。
8. 然則埃卡維達（Ekavi;;^a）乃一切讚歌之支柱，而尾羽乃一切鳥類之支柱。
9. 他誦讀一首二十二行的詩句。這被作為兩個支撐點的形式。因此所有的鳥類都以尾巴支撐自己，並且以尾巴支撐自己之後，牠們便飛起來。因為尾巴是一個支撐。
10. 他（鳥與讚歌）由兩個十年詩節所支持，即毗剌犮（Viraf）詩節。人（祭祀者）由兩個二足詩節所支持，即第二十一與第二十二詩節。構成鳥之物乃用以成就一切欲望；構成人之物，乃用以成就其幸福、榮耀、適切食物與尊榮。
11. 接著是蘇達多哈斯（Sudadohas）經句，然後是提亞雅（Dhayya），再後是蘇達多哈斯經句。蘇達多哈斯代表男性，提亞雅代表女性，因此他誦讀提亞雅時，將其視為被蘇達多哈斯從兩側擁抱著。因此，當兩者的精液射出時，便獲得了一體性，這關乎 ¹ 這些經句見於 Ait. Ar. V, 2, 2, 9。
1. Ima nu kam (梨俱吠陀 X, 157) .... 5 2. A yahi (梨俱吠陀 X, 172) 4 3. Pra va indraya 等 (薩卡爾亞本《吠陀》中無此內容) 9 4. Esha brahma 等 (薩卡爾亞本《吠陀》中無此內容) _3_ 2 阿耆尼索馬祭的其他讚頌文有特里普達、帕恰達、薩普達達，以埃卡維姆沙為最高級。參見卷一，5，1，3。
192 阿依塔雷亞‧阿蘭亞卡
女性而已。因此出生是在女性身上並從女性而來。所以他在那個地方誦唸那個《持者經》（Dhayya）¹。
第三篇章
1. 他誦唱了八十首蓋亞特里三行詩。2. 確實，這八十首蓋亞特里三行詩（Gayatri tristichs）就是這個世界（大地）。無論這個世界中有什麼榮光、偉大、妻子、食物與尊榮，願我獲得它，願我贏得它，願它屬於我。
2. 接著是《蘇陀陀訶》詩句。蘇陀陀訶確實是呼吸。他以呼吸連結此世界。
3. 他誦唱八十首布利哈蒂三行詩（Brz'hati）。誠然，八十首布利哈蒂三行詩乃是天空之世界。凡天空之世界所有的榮光、偉大、妻妾、食物與榮譽，願我獲得之，願我贏得之，願其屬於我。
4. 接著是蘇達多哈斯（Sudadohas）詩句。蘇達多哈斯確實是氣息。他以氣息連結天空的世界。
5. 他誦唸烏沙（Ushnh）的八十首三行詩。確實，這八十首烏什維（Ushwih）三行詩就是那個世界、天堂。凡是存在於那個榮耀之世界中的一切——偉大、妻子、食物與榮譽，以及提婆（Deva）的神聖存有（梵天）——願我獲得它，願我贏得它，願它成為我的。
6. 其次是《蘇陀多哈斯》詩句。蘇陀多哈斯（Sudadohas）誠然是呼吸。他以呼吸連結那個世界，是的，以呼吸連結。
阿斯敏 vi^-avabhage。註疏。
2 這些及以下的詩句構成了此鳥的食物。注疏文獻。這些詩句本身由沙烏納卡（Saunaka）在第五《阿蘭耶卡》（Aranyaka）中提供。
第一篇章
他誦唱瓦薩讚歌，並祈願道：「願萬物皆在我掌握之中。」
